content.rendered:
“Brand New Cadillac” was one of the first British rock and roll singles, first released in 1959 by Vince Taylor (as a B-side, actually).

Twenty years later, The Clash covered it and released it on London Calling, giving the original rockabilly arrangement a harder punk edge.

And then almost another twenty years on, Bruce Springsteen and The Wallflowers put their own spin on it when Bruce joined the band on stage at their gig at Tradewinds in Sea Bright, New Jersey.

For a man almost a year-and-a-half into a solo acoustic tour, Bruce seriously rocks it–it sounds like he’s having a blast.

From March 8, 1997, take a listen to Bruce and The Wallflowers tear it up on “Brand New Cadillac.”

Brand New Cadillac
First performed: March 8, 1997 (Sea Bright, NJ)
Last performed: March 8, 1997 (Seat Bright, NJ)

© February 7, 2019

excerpt.rendered: One time only: Bruce and The Wallflowers cover Vince Taylor’s 1959 rockabilly classic, “Brand New Cadillac.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: God Don’t Make Lonely Girls
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins The Wallflowers on their 1996 deep cut, "God Don't Make Lonely Girls," when the band's 1997 tour brings them to Bruce's neck of the woods.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Bring It on Home to Me
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins The Wallflowers in a gorgeous cover of Sam Cooke's classic "Bring It on Home to Me."

jetpack-related-posts.title: One Headlight
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce teams up with "The New Dylan" and his band on the MTV Video Awards.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Pink Cadillac
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: It may be pink, but it ain’t no car. Backstory and great performances inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Bette Midler: Pink Cadillac
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Bruce cover that never was: Bette Midler recorded her version of "Pink Cadillac," but Bruce refused to let her release it. Find out why and check out her live performance of it inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Nitty Gritty Dirt Band: Cadillac Ranch
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Two great live performances of "Cadillac Ranch" today--both by the Nitty Gritty Dirt Band.
